project_summary: Close two integration gaps against the Aitri Integration Contract v0.1.70: (1) Hub does not read BUGS.json (v0.1.67+) so open critical/high bugs are invisible in the dashboard; (2) Hub does not expose the lastSession field (v0.1.70+) from .aitri so session activity is lost. North Star KPI: zero integration contract fields missing from Hub collection. JTBD: developers hire Hub to know the full health of their Aitri projects without switching to the CLI. Guardrail metric: collection cycle time must not increase by more than 50ms per project.
no_go_zone: No BUGS.json writing or editing from Hub — Hub is strictly read-only; bug lifecycle is owned by Aitri CLI
No bug triage, assignment, or commenting UI in Hub — Hub surfaces counts and alerts only
No 04_CODE_REVIEW.md integration — deferred to a separate feature
No graph node changes (US/AC nodes) — deferred to graph product revision
No lastSession.context field rendering — too verbose for a dashboard card; only agent, event, and timestamp are displayed

functional_requirements.title: Collector reads BUGS.json and returns bug summary per project
functional_requirements.description: A new reader lib/collector/bugs-reader.js reads <artifactsDir>/BUGS.json and returns a compact summary: counts by status (open/fixed/verified/closed) and counts by severity (critical/high/medium/low), plus a list of open bug IDs. Returns null when the file is absent or malformed — no crash.
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid BUGS.json with 3 open bugs (1 critical, 1 high, 1 medium), readBugsSummary returns { open: 3, fixed: 0, verified: 0, closed: 0, critical: 1, high: 1, medium: 1, low: 0, openIds: ['BG-001','BG-002','BG-003'] }
Given an absent BUGS.json, readBugsSummary returns null without throwing
Given a malformed BUGS.json (invalid JSON), readBugsSummary returns null without throwing
readBugsSummary completes in ≤10ms for a file with up to 200 bugs

functional_requirements.title: Alerts engine generates alerts for open critical and high bugs
functional_requirements.description: The alerts engine evaluates bugsSummary and generates alerts: a blocking alert when any open critical or high bugs exist, a warning alert when only open medium/low bugs exist. No alert when bugsSummary is null or all bugs are fixed/verified/closed.
acceptance_criteria: Given bugsSummary.open > 0 and (bugsSummary.critical > 0 or bugsSummary.high > 0), evaluateAlerts generates exactly one alert with severity 'blocking' and message containing the counts
Given bugsSummary.open > 0 and bugsSummary.critical === 0 and bugsSummary.high === 0, evaluateAlerts generates exactly one alert with severity 'warning'
Given bugsSummary null or bugsSummary.open === 0, evaluateAlerts generates zero bug-related alerts
Alert message format: 'Open bugs: N critical, N high' for blocking; 'Open bugs: N medium/low' for warning

functional_requirements.title: Project cards in the Overview tab display open bug count badge
functional_requirements.description: Each project card in the Overview tab shows a bug count badge when bugsSummary.open > 0. The badge is color-coded: red for critical/high open bugs, yellow for medium/low only. No badge is shown when open count is 0 or bugsSummary is null.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project with 2 open bugs (1 critical), the project card shows a red badge with '2 bugs' within the card metrics area
Given a project with 1 open medium bug and no critical/high bugs, the card shows a yellow badge with '1 bug'
Given a project with 0 open bugs or null bugsSummary, no bug badge is rendered on the card
Bug badge renders within the existing card layout without increasing card height on viewport widths ≥768px

functional_requirements.title: aitri-reader exposes lastSession field from .aitri
functional_requirements.description: readAitriState returns a lastSession object when the field is present in .aitri: { at, agent, event, files_touched }. Returns lastSession: null when the field is absent (backward compatible with pre-v0.1.70 projects).
acceptance_criteria: Given .aitri with lastSession: { at, agent, event, files_touched }, readAitriState returns lastSession with all four fields preserved as-is
Given .aitri without a lastSession field, readAitriState returns lastSession: null without throwing
Given lastSession.files_touched absent, readAitriState returns lastSession.files_touched as null (not undefined)
lastSession.agent is returned as a string exactly as stored (no normalization)

functional_requirements.title: Activity tab displays lastSession per project
functional_requirements.description: The Activity tab renders a lastSession row for each project that has a non-null lastSession. Shows: agent name (color-coded by agent type), event string, and relative timestamp. Projects with null lastSession show no session row. Renders within the existing ActivityTab layout without layout shifts or new tabs.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project with lastSession.agent='claude', the Activity tab renders a row with a color-coded agent badge and event text within ≤100ms of data load
Given a project with lastSession: null, the Activity tab renders no lastSession row for that project and shows no empty placeholder
lastSession rows are visually distinct from pipeline event rows (different label/icon) so users can distinguish session checkpoints from phase events
Relative timestamp shown in the same format as existing event timestamps (e.g. '2h ago', '5m ago')

user_personas.goal: Monitor health of 5–15 Aitri-managed projects from one dashboard without switching to CLI
user_personas.pain_point: Open critical bugs and last-agent-session info are not visible in Hub — requires running aitri status per project to find them

user_stories.i_want: Hub to read each project's BUGS.json
user_stories.so_that: bug data is available in the dashboard without leaving Hub

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with a valid BUGS.json at <artifactsDir>/BUGS.json
acceptance_criteria.then: the project entry in dashboard.json includes bugsSummary with open/fixed/verified/closed and critical/high/medium/low counts
bugsSummary is null and collection succeeds without error

user_stories.i_want: Hub to alert me when a project has open critical or high bugs
user_stories.so_that: I can prioritize fixing blocking issues before they go unnoticed

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with 2 open bugs: 1 critical, 1 medium
acceptance_criteria.then: a blocking alert 'Open bugs: 1 critical, 0 high' is shown for that project
no bug-related alert is shown for that project

user_stories.i_want: to see a bug count badge on each project card
user_stories.so_that: I can spot projects with open blocking bugs from the Overview tab without opening Alerts

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with bugsSummary.open=2 and bugsSummary.critical=1
acceptance_criteria.then: the project card shows a red badge labeled '2 bugs'
no bug badge is rendered on the card and no layout shift occurs

user_stories.i_want: Hub to read the lastSession field from each project's .aitri
user_stories.so_that: session data is available without additional API calls

acceptance_criteria.given: a .aitri file with lastSession: { at, agent: 'claude', event: 'complete requirements' }
acceptance_criteria.then: aitriState.lastSession contains the correct at, agent, and event values

acceptance_criteria.given: a .aitri file from before v0.1.70 with no lastSession field
acceptance_criteria.then: aitriState.lastSession is null and no exception is thrown

user_stories.i_want: to see which agent last worked on each project and what they did
user_stories.so_that: I can coordinate with teammates and understand project momentum at a glance

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with lastSession.agent='claude' and lastSession.event='complete requirements'
acceptance_criteria.then: a row shows a 'claude' badge, 'complete requirements' text, and a relative timestamp like '3h ago'
no lastSession row is rendered for that project

non_functional_requirements.requirement: BUGS.json collection must not increase the per-project collection cycle time by more than 50ms
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: readBugsSummary completes in ≤10ms for files with up to 200 bug entries (synchronous file read)

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Absent, malformed, or empty BUGS.json must not affect collection of other project fields
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Given any BUGS.json error condition, collectOne returns the same fields it would return without BUGS.json support; collectionError remains null unless the .aitri file is also missing

non_functional_requirements.requirement: lastSession field must be backward-compatible with pre-v0.1.70 .aitri files
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Given a .aitri file with no lastSession field, readAitriState returns lastSession: null and all other fields are unaffected

constraints: Hub is read-only — it never writes to project directories or .aitri files
BUGS.json lives at <project>/<artifactsDir>/BUGS.json — path constructed using aitriState.artifactsDir ('' or 'spec')
Collection must remain a single synchronous pass per project — no new async I/O patterns
technology_preferences: Node.js built-in fs (synchronous) for BUGS.json reader — consistent with all other readers
React functional components for Activity tab lastSession rows — consistent with existing tab components
